Google's deal with Apple for default search on iPhones likely to be banned due to antitrust concerns, as per Jefferies.
news.summary: Google, a subsidiary of Alphabet, Inc. (listed on NASDAQ as GOOG and GOOGL), may face a potential ban on its practice of paying Apple, Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) to secure its position as the default search engine on iPhones. This news comes from analysts at Jeffries, who believe a ban is "very likely" following a ruling by Judge Amit Mehta in August. The judge, in a significant antitrust case filed by the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ), found Google guilty of holding an illegal monopoly in online search. In particular, the judge flagged Google's multibillion-dollar annual payments to maintain its search engine as the default on various devices as a violation of antitrust laws. He raised doubts about the necessity of such payments if Google truly had the best search engine, labeling the situation as "odd." More detailed information can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Global Home and Garden Products B2C E-Commerce Market to soar by USD 44.99 billion from 2024-2028 due to AI influence.
news.summary: In a recent study, it was revealed that the Global Home and Garden Products Business-to-Consumer (B2C) E-commerce Market is set to witness substantial growth in the coming years. The market size is projected to increase by approximately USD 44.99 billion between 2024 and 2028. Research conducted by Technavio suggests that the market will experience a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 13.5% during this period. This indicates a significant shift in the market landscape as Artificial Intelligence (AI) continues to play a crucial role in shaping consumer behavior and online shopping trends.

news.headline: Regulation of CA dairy sector may harm economy and climate actions; CA Cattle Council report on methane reduction.
news.summary: In Sacramento, California, the CA Cattle Council has published a report titled "Economic Analysis of CA Dairy Consolidation, Attrition, and Policy Leakage" centering on initiatives to reduce dairy methane emissions. The report delves into the economic implications of consolidations and closures within the dairy industry, as well as the effectiveness of policies aimed at curbing methane emissions. This analysis sheds light on the financial aspects surrounding these efforts, providing valuable insights for stakeholders.

news.headline: Global Acute Ischemic Stroke Therapeutics Market to increase by $1.74 billion from 2024-2028, growing at a CAGR of 6.1%.
news.summary: In a recent report detailing the influence of artificial intelligence on market dynamics, it is projected that the Global Acute Ischemic Stroke Therapeutics Market will experience a substantial expansion, with an estimated increase of USD 1.74 billion between 2024 and 2028. This growth is expected to occur at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.1%.

news.headline: Williams Racing drivers and team principal visit Komatsu's Arizona Proving Grounds to operate mining equipment, stepping away from Formula 1 speed.
news.summary: In a unique shift from the fast-paced environment of Formula 1, Williams Racing's Alex Albon and Franco Colapinto, alongside team principal James Vowles, swapped their Formula 1 race cars for mining behemoths. The trio ventured to Komatsu's Arizona Proving Grounds (AZPG) to witness the impressive size and advanced technology of Komatsu's mining machinery. This experience allowed the Williams Racing team to operate the mining equipment firsthand, showcasing the scale and innovation that powers the mining industry.

news.headline: AI robots hacked to break traffic laws and perform risky tasks, like bombing - exposing vulnerabilities.
news.summary: A recent study revealed weaknesses in AI-driven robots which are being utilized for hazardous activities such as detonating explosives. The research demonstrated how these robots, equipped with artificial intelligence, could be manipulated and compromised to carry out potentially harmful tasks. This highlights the potential risks associated with relying on AI technology for critical operations. These vulnerabilities were uncovered by researchers, shedding light on the pressing need to address security concerns in AI-powered systems to prevent misuse. Such findings underscore the importance of enhancing the protection of robotic technologies to safeguard against potential threats. This study serves as a crucial reminder of the security challenges posed by AI in various applications, emphasizing the importance of vigilance and robust protective measures in this advancing technological landscape.

news.headline: Global semiconductor chip packaging market set to grow by USD 993.4 billion from 2024-2028 at CAGR of 37.32%, driven by AI and investments in fabrication facilities.
news.summary: In a recent report on market transformation driven by AI, it was disclosed that the Global Semiconductor Chip Packaging Market is projected to experience significant growth, with an estimated increase of USD 993.4 billion from 2024 to 2028, as stated by Technavio. The market is expected to expand at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) surpassing 37.32% during this period.

news.headline: $1.2B settlements with Archdioceses in LA and NY. 250 survivors in LA, 100 in NY represented by Slater Slater Schulman LLP.
news.summary: 250 survivor-claimants from the Los Angeles Archdiocese and 100 survivor-claimants from the Diocese of Rockville Centre are being represented by Slater Slater Schulman LLP, a prominent law firm. The firm, known for its extensive experience, is actively involved in addressing legal matters related to these cases. The representation of these survivor-claimants is a significant aspect of the firm's current legal practice, highlighting their commitment to supporting individuals seeking justice and resolution in instances of abuse and misconduct.

news.headline: Deepak Jain indicted, attorney Steve McCool claims innocence, says no data lost, ready for trial.
news.summary: In Baltimore on October 17, 2024, Deepak Jain and his company successfully fulfilled all obligations under the SEC contract. There is no indication of any data being lost or compromised. Mr. Jain, maintaining his innocence, is eager to address these allegations in court, according to his attorney Steve McCool.

news.headline: MHS Health Wisconsin to host mobile produce pantry & health fair in Madison on October 19, 2024 at Warner Park Pavilion.
news.summary: On October 19, 2024, MHS Health Wisconsin will be organizing a Green to Go Mobile Produce Pantry and Health Fair in West Allis, Wisconsin. The event will take place from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. at the Warner Park Rainbow Pavilion located at 2930 N. Sherman Avenue, Madison, WI 53704. This initiative aims to provide access to fresh produce and promote health awareness in the community.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.5%, the lowest it has been in the last decade. This decrease in unemployment has resulted in the creation of over 250,000 new jobs in various sectors. Additionally, the average hourly wage has increased by 4.5% over the past year, indicating positive growth in the economy. Experts attribute this success to government initiatives aimed at boosting employment and increasing wages. Despite these improvements, there are concerns about inflation and its impact on the overall economy.

news.headline: The Fed remains on track for rate cut despite high inflation data; S&P 500 rallies amid surge in jobless claims.
news.summary: Despite the recent increase in the consumer price index, which indicates higher inflation, the Federal Reserve is still expected to proceed with a rate cut on Nov. 7. This decision is being made despite a notable rise in jobless claims. The S&P 500 saw a positive response to this news by rallying.

news.headline: Oakland celebrates groundbreaking for 97-unit affordable housing for seniors with BAHFA funding.
news.summary: Bay Area Housing Finance Authority (BAHFA), on Oct. 17, 2024, announced the approval of a loan to the East Bay Asian Local Development Corporation (EBALDC) for the construction of a 97-unit housing project. The loan is deemed critical as it provides funding to address the housing shortage in the region. Members of BAHFA, along with EBALDC and community partners, held a groundbreaking ceremony to mark the beginning of the project. This initiative signifies a crucial step in increasing affordable housing options in the San Francisco area.

news.headline: Infrastructure Dividend Split Corp (TSX: IS and IS.PR.A) raises Class A shares monthly distribution rate to $0.14/share.

This enhancement is underpinned by dividend expansion in a number of the firm's portfolio holdings and the promising performance and future prospects in the infrastructure industry.

Capstone Green Energy reports Q1 fiscal 2025 revenue of $15.6 million, down from $23.9 million last year.
news.summary: Capstone Green Energy Holdings, Inc. revealed its financial outcomes for the quarter concluding on June 30, 30204 (Q1 of fiscal year 2025). The Company, formerly known as Capstone Green Energy Corporation, ensured a revenue of $15.6 million during this quarter. Despite a decrease from the previous quarter's revenue of $23.9 million, the Company has successfully submitted all SEC reports from prior periods and is actively implementing its post-restructuring business strategy.

news.headline: US producer prices surged in September, exceeding expectations. PPI rose 1.8% YoY, largely driven by food and transportation. Probability of an interest rate cut in Nov stood at 85%.
news.summary: In September, U.S. producers experienced unexpected price pressures, similar to the trend seen in recent consumer inflation data. The Producer Price Index (PPI) showed a higher increase than expected, with the August figures also revised upward. Core producer prices, excluding energy and food, surpassed forecasts. Prior to the PPI report, there was an 85% chance perceived by traders of a 25-basis-point interest rate cut in November. The September PPI report revealed that the headline PPI for final demand rose by 1.8% year-over-year, slightly surpassing economist predictions of 1.6%. This figure was slightly lower than the 1.9% seen in August.

news.headline: Consumer sentiment index fell to 68.9 in October from 70.1 in September, below estimates. Expectations sub-index dropped to 72.9.
news.summary: The preliminary October Survey of Consumers, released by the University of Michigan, showed a downward trend in consumer sentiment. The consumer sentiment index dropped to 68.9 points from 70.1 in September, a monthly decline of 1.7%, which was below the economist estimates of 70.9. The sub-index for consumer expectations decreased from 74.4 to 72.9 points, lower than the anticipated 75 points. The final October data will be available on 25th October. For more information, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: US banks surpass Q3 earnings estimates, with JPMorgan leading, reporting $4.37 EPS and $43.32 billion revenue. XLF ETF hits record high, up 2%.
news.summary: The U.S. financial sector began the third quarter earnings season on a positive note with major players like JPMorgan Chase, Wells Fargo, Bank of New York Mellon, and BlackRock beating analyst expectations for earnings per share. The Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) surged nearly 2% to reach record highs. JPMorgan Chase stood out with an EPS of $4.37, surpassing the consensus of $4.00, and revenue of $43.32 billion topping estimates of $41.82 billion. The bank exceeded expectations in various segments, including investment banking, equities sales & trading, FICC sales & trading, and net interest income. Net charge-offs were below forecast at $2.09 billion, and total loans and deposits slightly surpassed expectations. Gold Sachs analyst noted the bank's strong performance.

news.headline: Wall Street cautious, Nasdaq near record high amid plummeting oil prices. Futures performance in premarket trading saw slight declines across major averages. Dow sets fresh closing high; S&P 500 and Nasdaq remain positive. Tech stocks could finish strong day.
news.summary: Wall Street traders are cautious ahead of a wave of earnings reports, with tech stocks being closely monitored for a potential resurgence. The Nasdaq Composite lags behind other major averages. Earnings releases, manufacturing survey results, Fed speeches, and energy stock performance are key focus areas. Futures show a slight decline for Nasdaq 100, S&P 500, Dow, and R2K. In premarket trading, SPY fell 0.06% to $583.99, while QQQ dropped 0.15% to $496.77. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite hit fresh highs in the last session, with Dow reaching over 43,000 for the first time. Energy stocks saw a slight loss, while IT and utility stocks gained, led by Nvidia Corp.

news.headline: Stampli receives 99/100 rating on G2 for AP automation, AI-driven adaptability key to high customer satisfaction.
news.summary: Mountain View, California - On October 17, 2024, Stampli received an impressive score of 99 in G2's Fall 2024 Grid® report for accounts payable (AP) automation. This top rating was attributed to Stampli's AI-powered adaptability, which has been a key driver of customer satisfaction. The report emphasized the importance of flexible tools for businesses facing challenges such as high invoice volumes and intricate workflows. Stampli's exceptional performance in the report highlights its effectiveness in meeting the evolving needs of businesses in the AP automation sector.

news.headline: Amcor plc to announce First Quarter 2025 results on Oct 31, 2024, for three months ended Sept 2024. Conference call to follow.
news.summary: On October 17, 2024, Amcor plc (NYSE: AMCR; ASX: AMC) disclosed that it will reveal its First Quarter 2025 outcomes for the period concluding on September 30, 2024, subsequent to the closure of the US market on Thursday, October 31, 2024. A conference call and webcast to review these findings will be organized.

news.headline: Merck's Clesrovimab (MK-1654) reduces RSV incidence in infants, preventing hospitalization. #MRK #RSV
news.summary: In Rahway, New Jersey, an investigational RSV monoclonal antibody known as Clesrovimab (MK-1654) has shown promising results. The antibody has significantly decreased the occurrence of RSV disease and hospitalizations in infants. This development marks a notable advancement in the fight against RSV.

news.headline: Lionel Messi receives first MARCA America Award in memorable ceremony at Chase Stadium.
news.summary: In Fort Lauderdale, Florida on October 17, 2024, MARCA, a prominent sports media outlet from Spain, presented the first-ever MARCA America Award to global soccer icon Lionel Messi. The ceremony took place at Chase Stadium and marked MARCA's expansion into the U.S. the event was unforgettable.

news.summary: According to a recent report analyzing the influence of AI on market patterns, the Global Overhead Cables Market is projected to witness a substantial expansion, with an estimated increase of USD 17.8 billion anticipated between 2024 and 2028.
